concepts.finding:
To
avoid the information leakage and efficiently train the bidirectional
model, we adopt the Cloze objective to sequential recommendation,
predicting the random masked items in the sequence by jointly
conditioning on their left and right context.

To the
best of our knowledge, this is the first study to introduce
deep bidirectional sequential model and Cloze objective into
the field of recommendation systems.

For sequential recom-
mendation, Kang and McAuley [22] introduce a two-layer Trans-
former decoder (i.e., Transformer language model) called SASRec
to capture user’s sequential behaviors and achieve state-of-the-art
results on several public datasets.

concepts.finding:
In order to efficiently train our proposed model, we apply a
new objective: Cloze task [50] (also known as “Masked Language
Model” in [6]) to sequential recommendation.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
Although our BERT4Rec is inspired by the BERT in NLP,
it still has several differences from BERT: a) The most critical dif-
ference is that BERT4Rec is an end-to-end model for sequential
recommendation, while BERT is a pre-training model for sentence
representation.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
4
EXPERIMENTS
4.1
Datasets
We evaluate the proposed model on four real-world representative
datasets which vary significantly in domains and sparsity.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
• SASRec [22]: It uses a left-to-right Transformer language
model to capture users’ sequential behaviors, and achieves
state-of-the-art performance on sequential recommendation.
concepts.implication: Architecture pattern applicable to sequence modeling for user behavior

concepts.finding:
The results show that BERT4Rec with 1 mask
significantly outperforms SASRec on all metrics.
